topic.name: Animal Health Law — equine identification & databases (corpus)
topic.description: Curated equine-focused excerpts from Regulation (EU) 2016/429: Article 109 database fields for equidae, Article 114 equine identification, Article 110 identification documents cross-cut, plus UK retained assimilated presentations where published on legislation.gov.uk. Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2015/262 and other AHL-linked instruments appear as source-family discovery candidates (see corpus_equine_law.json / merge output) until explicitly registered and added here as sources.

cluster.name: animal_identification_traceability
cluster.description: Identification, registration, establishments, and movement-related record duties.

sources.title: Regulation (EU) 2016/429 — Article 109 (database of kept terrestrial animals)

metadata.capture_note: Substantive text aligned to legislation.gov.uk /eur/2016/429/article/109 plain view; EU authoritative wording may differ editorially on EUR-Lex.

sources.title: Regulation (EU) 2016/429 (retained on legislation.gov.uk) — Article 109
Regulation (EU) 2016/429 — Article 114 (equine identification and registration)
sources.text:
Article 114
Operators' obligations in respect of the identification and registration of kept animals of the equine species
1.
Operators keeping kept animals of the equine species shall ensure that those animals are individually identified by:
(a)
a unique code which is recorded in the computer database provided for in Article 109(1);
(b)
a physical means of identification or other method which unequivocally links the kept animal with the identification document provided for in point (c) of this paragraph and issued by the competent authority in accordance with Article 110;
(c)
a correctly completed single lifetime identification document.
2.
Operators of kept animals of the equine species shall ensure that the information on those animals is transmitted to the computer database provided for in Article 109(1).

sources.title: Regulation (EU) 2016/429 — Article 114 (equine identification and registration)
sources.text:
Article 114
Operators' obligations in respect of the identification and registration of kept animals of the equine species
1.
Operators keeping kept animals of the equine species shall ensure that those animals are individually identified by:
(a)
a unique code which is recorded in the computer database provided for in Article 109(1);
(b)
a physical means of identification or other method which unequivocally links the kept animal with the identification document provided for in point (c) of this paragraph and issued by the competent authority in accordance with Article 110;
(c)
a correctly completed single lifetime identification document.
2.
Operators of kept animals of the equine species shall ensure that the information on those animals is transmitted to the computer database provided for in Article 109(1).

sources.title: Regulation (EU) 2016/429 — Article 110 (identification documents — terrestrial)
sources.text:
Article 110
Obligation of the competent authority in respect of identification documents, movement documents and other documents for the identification and tracing of kept terrestrial animals
1.
Each competent authority shall:
(a)
issue identification documents in respect of kept terrestrial animals where those documents are required by point (c) of Article 114(1) and point (b) of Article 117 and by rules adopted pursuant to Articles 118 and 120;
(b)
issue identification documents in respect of bovine animals as required by point (b) of Article 112, unless Member States exchange electronic data with other Member States within the framework of an electronic exchange system from the date when the Commission recognises the full operability of that system;
(c)
draw up models of movement documents and other documents for the identification and tracing of kept terrestrial animals, when required by point (b) of Article 113(1), point (b) of Article 115, point (b) of Article 117 and any rules adopted pursuant to Articles 118 and 120.
2.
Point (b) of paragraph (1) is without prejudice to the right of Member States to adopt national rules on the issuing of passports for animals not intended for movement between Member States.

metadata.capture_note: Plain-structure capture aligned to legislation.gov.uk /eur/2016/429/article/110 revised view 2024-12-24; verify against EUR-Lex consolidated CELEX 02016R0429 where EU-authoritative wording required.

sources.title: Regulation (EU) 2016/429 (retained on legislation.gov.uk) — Article 110
sources.text:
Article 110
Obligation of the competent authority in respect of identification documents, movement documents and other documents for the identification and tracing of kept terrestrial animals
1.
Each competent authority shall:
(a)
issue identification documents in respect of kept terrestrial animals where those documents are required by point (c) of Article 114(1) and point (b) of Article 117 and by rules adopted pursuant to Articles 118 and 120;
(b)
issue identification documents in respect of bovine animals as required by point (b) of Article 112, unless Member States exchange electronic data with other Member States within the framework of an electronic exchange system from the date when the Commission recognises the full operability of that system;
(c)
draw up models of movement documents and other documents for the identification and tracing of kept terrestrial animals, when required by point (b) of Article 113(1), point (b) of Article 115, point (b) of Article 117 and any rules adopted pursuant to Articles 118 and 120.
2.
Point (b) of paragraph (1) is without prejudice to the right of Member States to adopt national rules on the issuing of passports for animals not intended for movement between Member States.

metadata.capture_note: Plain-structure capture aligned to legislation.gov.uk /eur/2016/429/article/110 revised view 2024-12-24; verify against EUR-Lex consolidated CELEX 02016R0429 where EU-authoritative wording required.

narrative.title: Equine AHL corpus slice (109 / 114 / 110)
narrative.summary: Expanded from the Article 109 pilot: paired EU vs UK-retained Article 109 and 114, plus concrete Article 110 text invoked by Article 114(1)(b)-(c). Not exhaustive equine law.

run_notes: equine_law_corpus_case.json — curated corpus sources only (no developer fixtures). Coverage is intentionally narrow; delegated-rule references (Articles 118/120 etc.) appear as textual hooks only until separately sourced. Source-family discovery (merged at export) lists 2015/262, 2019/2035, 2021/963, 2020/688, 2020/692 etc. as candidates — not analysed until registered sources are added.
